subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the thematic message of the poem 'Harvest Hymn'
Interpret the symbolic roles of Surya, Varuna, and Prithvi in the poem
Examine how poetic devices such as imagery, repetition, metaphor, and personification enhance the poem
Understand how the poem reflects the relationship between humans, nature, and the divine
Evaluate the significance of community participation in harvest celebrations
Develop higher-order reading comprehension, interpretation, and analytical writing skills

lesson_plan.title: Cultural Context of Harvest and Hymns
topics_covered: Meaning of harvest in agricultural societies
Understanding hymns as songs of praise
teaching_strategy: Presentation of examples of global harvest celebrations
Think–pair–share discussion on gratitude in agriculture
student_activities: Research and share examples of harvest festivals in India
Discuss why societies celebrate harvest through songs and rituals

lesson_plan.title: Poet and Historical Context
topics_covered: Life and works of Sarojini Naidu
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of Sarojini Naidu’s contribution to literature
Close reading of the poem’s language
student_activities: Rewrite archaic words in modern English
Discuss how archaic language affects the tone of the poem

lesson_plan.title: Men’s Voices – Natural Forces and Agriculture
topics_covered: Symbolic representation of Surya and Varuna
Agricultural dependence on sunlight and rainfall
Imagery related to fields and crops
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of imagery and symbolism
student_activities: Identify imagery describing harvest and nature
Analyze how Surya and Varuna contribute to agricultural prosperity

lesson_plan.title: Women’s Voices – Earth as a Nurturing Mother
topics_covered: Role of fertile land in agriculture
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of personification and metaphor
Discussion on the nurturing role of Earth
student_activities: Identify literary devices used in the stanza
Discuss how personification strengthens the emotional impact of the poem

lesson_plan.title: All Voices – Unity, Symbolism, and Theme
topics_covered: Unity of voices in praising the divine
Philosophical message of harmony between humans and nature
student_activities: Interpret the symbolic meaning of 'Seed and the Scythe'
Write an analytical paragraph explaining the poem’s central theme

lesson_plan.title: Literary Analysis and Revision
topics_covered: Poetic devices: imagery, metaphor, alliteration, and repetition
Revision of poem themes and symbolism
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation with examples from the poem
student_activities: Identify poetic devices in selected lines
Write a short reflective essay on the poem’s message

teaching_aids: Images of harvest festivals and agricultural fields
evaluation_methods: Analytical paragraph and short essay writing
teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze the deeper symbolism of the poem and relate it to real-world agricultural practices. Discuss how gratitude toward nature remains relevant in modern environmental awareness.
